_note: Feature tables for the cyberpunk classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: Casts wizard-list spells as intrusion programs using INT; needs their cyberdeck as a focus.
May run any known program with the ritual tag over 10 minutes without spending a slot.

2.d: 1/rest, listen in on any electronic or magical communication within 100 ft.

3.d: Advantage on Stealth against cameras, sensors, and constructs.

5.d: Stores one program of level 1-3; cast it once without a slot per long rest.

6.d: Reaction: impose disadvantage on an attack against an ally you can see, 2/rest.

9.d: Project senses through any networked device within 1 mile for 10 minutes.

11.d: Cache holds two programs of level 1-5.

13.d: Learn two programs from any spell list; they count as wizard-list for you.

15.d: Advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saves against spells and psionics.

18.d: 1/long rest, control a construct or networked system for 1 minute (INT save negates).

20.d: Regain 4 expended slot levels on a short rest, once per day.

2.d: When a hostile program or spell targets you, reaction to add +2 AC or +2 to the save.
1/turn, a damaging program deals +1d8 lightning damage to one target.

6.d: When you succeed on a save against a spell, deal 2d8 psychic damage to the caster.

10.d: Your programs score a critical on 19-20 against constructs and spellcasters.

14.d: 1/long rest, split one single-target damaging program among up to three targets.

1.d: +2 to initiative; cannot be surprised while conscious.
Pick one: +2 to ranged attack rolls, +1 AC in light or medium armor, or +2 melee damage.

2.d: 1/short rest, take one additional action on your turn.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: +1 AC while not wearing heavy armor.

7.d: Bonus action: mark a foe you can see; your attacks against it deal +2 damage.

9.d: 1/long rest, reroll a failed saving throw.

11.d: Attack three times when taking the Attack action.

13.d: Reaction: +3 AC against one ranged attack you can see, 3/long rest.

14.d: Adrenal Override recharges twice per short rest.

15.d: Advantage on CON saves; resistance to poison damage.

17.d: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on 19-20.

18.d: 1/long rest while below half HP, bonus action to regain 10 + level HP.

20.d: 1/turn, dropping a foe to 0 HP grants one extra weapon attack immediately.

3.d: Two-weapon fighting with blades adds your ability mod to off-hand damage; +1 AC while dual-wielding.

7.d: Replace one attack with a spinning slash: weapon damage to every foe within 5 ft.

11.d: Dash or Disengage as a bonus action; opportunity attacks against you have disadvantage.

15.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute make one additional blade attack on each of your turns.

1.d: A small drone obeys you: AC 13, HP 5 + level, moves 30 ft, acts on your command.
2/long rest, 10 minutes of tinkering restores a broken device or gives a tool user +1d4 for 1 hour.

2.d: Casts wizard-list spells as drone routines using INT; the drone is the focus.
See and hear through your drone while it is within 100 ft.

5.d: A second drone; command all your drones with a single action.

6.d: Drone command and Remote Eyes range extends to 1 mile.

7.d: Your drones add your INT modifier to their damage.

9.d: Each drone regains 2d6 HP at the start of your turn, once per rest each.

10.d: Advantage on saves against effects that would sever or hijack control of your drones.

11.d: Your drones improve to AC 16 and HP 15 + twice your level.

13.d: Rebuild a destroyed drone on a long rest for free, or in 1 minute once per day.

14.d: Jury-Rig has 4 uses and can affect magical or exotic devices.

15.d: A third drone joins your swarm.

17.d: Command your drones as a bonus action.

18.d: 1/long rest, one drone turns invisible for 1 minute.

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your drones act independently and you may cast one routine through each.

3.d: Your primary drone mounts a weapon: on your command it attacks for 1d8 + INT force damage.

7.d: Your drones take half damage from area effects, none on a successful DEX save.

11.d: Two drones attack when you command an attack.

15.d: Primary drone doubles its HP, deals +1d8 damage, and can carry a rider.

1.d: 1/long rest, call in a favor: gear, a ride, a name, or entry somewhere (DM adjudicates scope).
1/short rest, reroll a failed CHA-based check.

2.d: Double proficiency bonus in two skills you are proficient with.
Disengage or Hide as a bonus action.

5.d: Street Contacts has 2 uses and reaches contacts in other cities.

6.d: Sell goods at full value; you can always find a buyer or seller within a day.

7.d: When you roll initiative, move up to your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

9.d: 1 hour of canvassing reveals a named local's whereabouts or recent dealings.

10.d: Double proficiency bonus in two more proficient skills.

11.d: 1/week, a contact performs a genuinely dangerous service for you.

13.d: A creature that accepts your parley cannot attack you until your next turn.

14.d: Grease the Wheels recharges to 3 uses per short rest.

15.d: Street Contacts has 3 uses; favors arrive in half the usual time.

17.d: 1/long rest, the DM tells you one useful secret about a person you converse with.

18.d: You and allies within 30 ft add +1d4 to CHA-based checks.

20.d: 1/long rest, declare a contact anywhere; one favor within reason simply succeeds.

3.d: After 1 minute observing a creature, learn one stat, weakness, or resistance of it.
Advantage on Investigation checks to gather information.

7.d: Advantage on CHA checks against anyone whose secret you know.

11.d: You always learn when someone in the city asks about you or your crew.

15.d: 1/long rest, ask the DM three questions about local people, places, or events; get brief true answers.

1.d: Casts sorcerer-list spells as psionics using CHA; needs no material focus.
1/short rest, bonus action: +2 AC until the start of your next turn.

2.d: Regain one 1st-level slot on a short rest.

3.d: An invisible telekinetic hand within 30 ft manipulates objects up to 10 lb.

5.d: 2/rest, cast a cantrip as a bonus action after casting a leveled psionic.

7.d: Resistance to falling damage; 1/turn, shove a target 10 ft with a psionic hit.

9.d: Your mind cannot be read; +2 on WIS saves.

15.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your CHA modifier.

17.d: 1/long rest, your next damaging psionic deals maximum damage.

18.d: Immune to psychic damage and mind reading.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast a psionic of 5th level or lower without a slot at two targets.

1.d: 1/turn, add your CHA modifier as psychic damage to a damaging psionic.

6.d: Reaction when a creature damages you: it takes psychic damage equal to your CHA modifier.

10.d: 1/long rest, a creature hit by your psionic saves (WIS) or is stunned until your next turn.

14.d: Resistance to psychic damage; advantage against charm and fear.

1.d: Bonus action touch: an ally regains 1d6 + WIS HP. Uses per long rest equal WIS modifier.
Assess a creature to learn its rough HP, conditions, and diseases.

2.d: Casts cleric-list spells as injected nanomeds using WIS.

5.d: Stabilize as a bonus action; creatures you stabilize wake with 1 HP after 1 minute.

7.d: You and allies within 5 ft have advantage against poison and disease.

9.d: An ally you stim can also stand up and move half speed for free.

10.d: 1/long rest, an ally at 0 HP within 30 ft instantly regains 3d6 + WIS HP.

11.d: Stim Injector heals 3d6 + WIS and can be fired as a dart at 30 ft.

13.d: 1/day, regain one expended spell slot of 3rd level or lower on a short rest.

14.d: You and allies within 10 ft have advantage against being frightened.

15.d: While below half HP, regain WIS-mod HP at the start of your turn (5 rounds per long rest).

17.d: Your healing dice each count as at least half their maximum.

18.d: 1/short rest, touch cures any disease, poison, blindness, deafness, or paralysis.

20.d: 1/week, restore a creature dead less than 1 minute to life at half HP.

3.d: Dash or Disengage as a bonus action when moving toward a downed ally.
While adjacent to a downed ally, attacks against you have disadvantage.

7.d: Carry or drag allies at full speed; allies you stabilize or heal gain 1d6 temp HP.

11.d: An ally you heal gains +2 AC until the start of your next turn.

15.d: 1/long rest, an ally within 30 ft that would drop to 0 HP drops to 1 instead.
